summary: Story 1.11 is implementation-ready. The draft is traceable to PRD Story 1.11, the validation reporting architecture, framework docs, the SM-to-PO handoff, Laravel Boost Filament/Laravel documentation, route discovery, and current memory model/service/admin code. PO accepted docs/validation/mvp-runbook.md as the runbook path and narrowed the required operator surface to a Filament Admin page, with an internal command optional only if Dev finds a concrete need.

goal_context_clarity.notes: The product goal, business value, Epic 1 closure role, Story 1.10 dependency, MVP validation scope, and privacy boundary are explicit.

technical_implementation_guidance.notes: The reporting service, required Filament Admin page, optional event table, optional command extension, and runbook path are specified with current implementation context.

reference_effectiveness.notes: Source references point to PRD, architecture, framework docs, handoff context, Laravel Boost docs, routes, models, services, migrations, and existing admin test patterns.

self_containment.notes: The story summarizes required metrics, aggregate-only output, disallowed private fields, admin-only access, derived-metric preference, and runbook boundaries in-story.

testing_guidance.notes: Focused service, Filament page, route/profile gate, private-content exclusion, optional command, and repository gate commands are listed.

coderabbit_integration.notes: CodeRabbit is enabled in core config; story type, agents, quality gates, self-healing configuration, and focus areas are populated and aligned with the approved scope.

executor_assignment.notes: Executor @dev and quality gate @architect are appropriate and distinct for a service/Admin/privacy/documentation slice. Quality gate commands now match the required Filament Admin surface.

anti_hallucination.notes: No unsupported customer-facing analytics, public dashboard, new BI dependency, automatic email ingestion, AI analysis, content translation, or per-record visibility model was introduced.

nice_to_have: During implementation, Dev should first prove whether existing memory_profiles, memory_records, tenant_user, media, and workspace settings data cover all required metrics before adding memory_activity_events.
If Dev adds an optional Artisan command, add tests proving command output is aggregate-only and excludes private content.

risk_notes: The main implementation risk is leaking private record fields while trying to make validation data useful. The story explicitly restricts output and event metadata to aggregate-safe fields.
The secondary risk is overbuilding analytics or post-MVP tracking. The story requires a lightweight Filament Admin page and runbook only.
